title: cynometra capuronii
intro: Cynometra capuronii, also known as the Madagascar ironwood, is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which is rich in flavonoids and tannins, and has been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments. The health benefits of Cynometra capuronii include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the bark is typically dried and made into a tea or infused in hot water to extract its medicinal compounds.
